Focus: races in checkpoint.py (pool cache, per-conn locks, atexit), the reasoning ThreadPoolExecutor (_inflight accounting, slot release, shutdown), context-manager/handle/connection close on exception paths, unbounded caches. 3 passes hardened — find residual issues. Return JSON: id (C-N), severity, file, line, title, description, poc, suggested_fix.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).

- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

1	"""Durable run-state checkpointer — local, data-sovereign (ADR-0003).
2	
3	The orchestration graph persists its full state (current phase, gate decisions, locked
4	hypotheses, verdicts) through a checkpointer so a run can interrupt at a human-approval gate
5	and resume later. That state holds the entire analysis, so the store is **on-box only**
6	(ADR-0003); a SaaS checkpointer is disqualified.
7	
8	This module is the LangGraph checkpointer factory, ported faithfully from the prior
9	platform's hardened implementation and generalized (dataset-agnostic ``ASSAY_*`` env, no
10	config-file coupling — see ADR-0006). The hardened behaviors are deliberately preserved
11	because each prevents a real production failure:
12	
13	- a **self-healing ConnectionPool** (not a raw connection): when Postgres goes away — node
14	  sleep, restart, idle timeout, a network blip — a cached raw connection is permanently
15	  broken and every later checkpoint op raises, i.e. durability is lost exactly during the
16	  interrupt/resume the checkpointer exists to support. The pool runs a liveness check on
17	  checkout and replaces dead connections;
18	- a **session advisory lock** serializing first-time schema setup across *processes*, run on
19	  the *same* connection as the DDL (a pool would otherwise check out a different connection,
20	  leaving the lock ineffective);
21	- a **once-per-process-per-conn guard** (``setup()`` is idempotent but re-locking + replaying
22	  the migration query on every call is wasteful), with an in-process lock making the
23	  check-and-set atomic so concurrent threads don't both run setup;
24	- **credential redaction**: a backend exception (psycopg/psycopg_pool) can embed the full
25	  ``user:password`` URI/DSN in its own message, so the conn string is scrubbed precisely and
26	  the exception text via URI-userinfo + DSN-password backstops; the redacted message is then
27	  raised *outside* the exception handler, so the credential-bearing original is retained as
28	  neither ``__cause__`` nor ``__context__`` (a logger walking ``__context__`` cannot
29	  re-expose it);
30	- a single shared **atexit** pool-cleanup handler (one per process, not one per call) so the
31	  pool's worker thread is joined before interpreter finalization.
32	
33	LangGraph/psycopg are optional (the ``persistence`` extra) and imported lazily; the
34	credential-redaction and connection-resolution logic is pure and unit-tested offline.
35	"""
36	
37	from __future__ import annotations
38	
39	import atexit
40	import os
41	import re
42	import threading
43	from typing import Any, cast
44	from urllib.parse import urlsplit
45	
46	from assay_engine._local import require_local_uri
47	
48	_DEFAULT_PG = "postgresql://localhost:5432/assay"
49	
50	# Arbitrary fixed key for the Postgres session advisory lock that serializes checkpoint
51	# schema migrations across concurrent initializers. The value is irrelevant beyond being a
52	# stable per-logical-schema constant; "ASSY" as an int.
53	_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY = 0x4153_5359
54	
55	# Upper bound (milliseconds) on how long the cross-process advisory lock may be waited for
56	# during schema bootstrap. A stuck/crashed peer that still holds a live PG session on the lock
57	# must NOT be able to hang bootstrap forever (#129); lock_timeout aborts the wait. 0 disables
58	# the bound (block indefinitely, the pre-#129 behavior). Read fresh on each bootstrap (#F-033) so
59	# an env var set after import still takes effect; the module constant is the default + the value
60	# tests monkeypatch.
61	_MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS = max(0, int(os.environ.get("ASSAY_MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS", "30000")))
62	
63	
64	def _migration_lock_timeout_ms() -> int:
65	    """The advisory-lock wait bound, re-read from the env each call (#F-033).
66	
67	    Falls back to the import-time module constant when the env var is unset, so a test that
68	    monkeypatches ``_MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS`` still drives the value.
69	    """
70	    raw = os.environ.get("ASSAY_MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS")
71	    if raw is None:
72	        return _MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS
73	    return max(0, int(raw))
74	
75	
76	# Brief meta-lock guarding the process-local registries below (atexit registration, the open-pool
77	# list, the per-conn lock map, the pool cache). It is held ONLY for in-memory check-and-set — it
78	# is NEVER held across blocking cross-process I/O (pool.connection(), the advisory-lock wait, or
79	# setup() DDL). That blocking work is serialized per conn_str instead (#129), so a stuck advisory
80	# lock on one database cannot block initialization of an unrelated one.
81	_init_lock = threading.Lock()
82	
83	# Per-conn_str bootstrap locks: only same-conn_str initializers serialize; different databases
84	# bootstrap concurrently. Created lazily under _init_lock.
85	_CONN_INIT_LOCKS: dict[str, threading.Lock] = {}
86	
87	# Live pools cached by conn_str so the factory reuses one self-healing pool (and its worker
88	# thread) per database rather than opening a fresh pool on every call (#144).
89	_POOLS_BY_CONN: dict[str, Any] = {}
90	
91	# Pools opened by the factory, closed once at process exit via a single shared atexit handler
92	# (registering one handler per call would accumulate handlers across repeated factory calls).
93	_OPEN_POOLS: list[Any] = []
94	_atexit_registered = False
95	
96	# Strips ``://[REDACTED]@`` (URI userinfo) from any URI-like substring. '/' is allowed
97	# inside the userinfo so a password containing '/' is still redacted; only '@' (the userinfo
98	# terminator) and whitespace bound the match.
99	_CREDS_RE = re.compile(r"://[REDACTED]@\s]+@")
100	# Strips a libpq keyword/value DSN password token (``password=[REDACTED] / ``pgpassword=...``),
101	# handling quoted values that may contain spaces.
102	_DSN_PW_RE = re.compile(r"(?i)\b(password|pgpassword)\s*=\s*('[^']*'|\"[^\"]*\"|\S+)")
103	
104	
105	def _sanitize_conn_str(conn_str: str) -> str:
106	    """Return ``scheme://host[:port]/path`` with any ``user:password`` userinfo stripped.
107	
108	    Robust to malformed userinfo: a password containing ``/``/``#`` makes ``urlsplit``'s
109	    ``.port`` raise ``ValueError`` on access, so the whole parse (including attribute access)
110	    is guarded and falls back to the generic userinfo-strip regex / a constant placeholder.
111	    """
112	    try:
113	        u = urlsplit(conn_str)
114	        host = u.hostname or ""
115	        port = u.port
116	        scheme = u.scheme
117	        path = u.path
118	    except ValueError:
119	        return _CREDS_RE.sub("://", conn_str)
120	    if not host:
121	        return _CREDS_RE.sub("://", conn_str) if scheme else "<store>"
122	    if ":" in host:  # IPv6 literal — urlsplit drops the brackets; restore them
123	        host = f"[{host}]"
124	    netloc = f"{host}:{port}" if port else host
125	    return f"{scheme}://{netloc}{path}" if scheme else "<store>"
126	
127	
128	def redact_creds(text: str, conn_str: str = "") -> str:
129	    """Remove credentials a backend exception may embed in its message text.
130	
131	    Layers, in order of precision:
132	
133	    1. exact replacement of the known ``conn_str`` (handles any password characters, incl.
134	       ``/`` and spaces) — this is the precise layer and covers the real connection string;
135	    2. a generic URI-userinfo strip (``://[REDACTED]@`` → ``://``) as a backstop for any
136	       *other* URI in the text;
137	    3. a libpq DSN ``password=[REDACTED] strip for the keyword/value form.
138	
139	    Layers 2–3 are best-effort for *secondary* endpoints (e.g. a replica DSN) the caller did
140	    not pass as ``conn_str``; the primary connection string is always scrubbed precisely.
141	    """
142	    if conn_str:
143	        text = text.replace(conn_str, _sanitize_conn_str(conn_str))
144	    text = _CREDS_RE.sub("://", text)
145	    return _DSN_PW_RE.sub(r"\1=***", text)
146	
147	
148	def get_postgres_connection_string() -> str:
149	    """Resolve the Postgres connection string (``ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL`` env → loopback default).
150	
151	    The env path bypasses any external config validation, so loopback is enforced here at the
152	    point of use (ADR-0003 defense-in-depth): graph state must not leave the box.
153	    """
154	    url = os.environ.get("ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL") or _DEFAULT_PG
155	    return require_local_uri(url, what="checkpointer connection")
156	
157	
158	def _safe_close(pool: Any) -> None:
159	    """Close one pool, swallowing errors so a single failing ``close()`` does not abort
160	    cleanup of the remaining pools at interpreter exit (``close()`` calls a C ``finish()``
161	    that can raise)."""
162	    try:
163	        pool.close()
164	    except Exception:
165	        pass
166	
167	
168	def _close_all_pools() -> None:
169	    # Snapshot under the meta-lock before iterating (#F-020): _OPEN_POOLS is appended under
170	    # _init_lock elsewhere, and on a free-threaded (GIL-less) build a concurrent list.append
171	    # reallocation during iteration is undefined behavior. _safe_close never takes _init_lock, so
172	    # closing outside the lock cannot deadlock.
173	    with _init_lock:
174	        pools = list(_OPEN_POOLS)
175	    for p in pools:
176	        _safe_close(p)
177	
178	
179	def _register_pool_cleanup(pool: Any) -> None:
180	    global _atexit_registered
181	    with _init_lock:
182	        _OPEN_POOLS.append(pool)
183	        if not _atexit_registered:
184	            atexit.register(_close_all_pools)
185	            _atexit_registered = True
186	
187	
188	def _conn_init_lock(conn_str: str) -> threading.Lock:
189	    """Return the per-conn_str bootstrap lock, creating it under the brief meta-lock (#129)."""
190	    with _init_lock:
191	        lock = _CONN_INIT_LOCKS.get(conn_str)
192	        if lock is None:
193	            lock = _CONN_INIT_LOCKS[conn_str] = threading.Lock()
194	        return lock
195	
196	
197	def _cached_pool(conn_str: str) -> Any:
198	    with _init_lock:
199	        return _POOLS_BY_CONN.get(conn_str)
200	
201	
202	def _acquire_migration_lock(conn: Any) -> None:
203	    """Acquire the cross-process schema-migration advisory lock with a bounded wait (#129).
204	
205	    ``lock_timeout`` aborts the ``pg_advisory_lock`` wait if a peer holds it past the deadline,
206	    so a stuck/crashed initializer cannot hang bootstrap indefinitely; the resulting error
207	    propagates as a redacted ``RuntimeError`` like any other backend failure. set_config is used
208	    (not ``SET``) so the value can be safely parameterized.
209	    """
210	    timeout_ms = _migration_lock_timeout_ms()
211	    if timeout_ms:
212	        conn.execute("SELECT set_config('lock_timeout', %s, false)", (str(timeout_ms),))
213	    conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_lock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
214	
215	
216	def get_checkpointer(use_memory: bool = False) -> Any:
217	    """Return a configured LangGraph checkpointer.
218	
219	    ``use_memory=True`` returns an in-memory saver (tests / no-Postgres). Otherwise a
220	    Postgres-backed saver over a self-healing pool, with advisory-locked one-time schema
221	    setup. Raises ``RuntimeError`` (with credentials redacted) if the backend is unreachable
222	    or the extra is not installed.
223	    """
224	    try:
225	        if use_memory:
226	            from langgraph.checkpoint.memory import MemorySaver
227	
228	            return MemorySaver()
229	        from langgraph.checkpoint.postgres import PostgresSaver
230	        from psycopg.rows import dict_row
231	        from psycopg_pool import ConnectionPool
232	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
233	        raise RuntimeError(
234	            "Postgres checkpointer requires the 'persistence' extra "
235	            "(langgraph-checkpoint-postgres, psycopg[binary,pool]) — not installed"
236	        ) from exc
237	
238	    conn_str = get_postgres_connection_string()
239	
240	    # Fast path: reuse a live, self-healing pool for this conn_str rather than opening a new pool
241	    # (and its background worker thread) on every call (#144). The liveness check on checkout
242	    # replaces dead connections, so a cached pool stays valid across transient outages.
243	    cached = _cached_pool(conn_str)
244	    if cached is not None:
245	        return PostgresSaver(cast(Any, cached))
246	
247	    # Serialize bootstrap PER conn_str — NOT process-globally (#129). Unrelated databases
248	    # initialize concurrently, and a stuck advisory lock on one conn_str can never block init of
249	    # another. The brief meta-lock (_init_lock) is only taken inside the helpers, never held
250	    # across the blocking advisory-lock wait / DDL below.
251	    with _conn_init_lock(conn_str):
252	        cached = _cached_pool(conn_str)  # another thread may have built it while we waited
253	        if cached is not None:
254	            return PostgresSaver(cast(Any, cached))
255	
256	        failure: str | None = None
257	        pool: Any = None
258	        try:
259	            # Pool bounds are env-tunable (#F-040): the reasoning seam already runs 8 worker
260	            # threads, so a fixed max_size=8 can be saturated under concurrent reasoning+checkpoint
261	            # load on larger hardware. connect_timeout bounds an individual TCP connect so a
262	            # black-holed host fails fast instead of hanging bootstrap for the OS default.
263	            min_size = max(0, int(os.environ.get("ASSAY_POOL_MIN_SIZE", "1")))
264	            max_size = max(min_size or 1, int(os.environ.get("ASSAY_POOL_MAX_SIZE", "8")))
265	            connect_timeout = max(1, int(os.environ.get("ASSAY_POOL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT", "10")))
266	            pool = ConnectionPool(
267	                conn_str,
268	                min_size=min_size,
269	                max_size=max_size,
270	                kwargs={
271	                    "autocommit": True,
272	                    "prepare_threshold": 0,
273	                    "row_factory": dict_row,
274	                    "connect_timeout": connect_timeout,
275	                },
276	                check=ConnectionPool.check_connection,  # liveness check on checkout (self-healing)
277	                open=True,
278	            )
279	            _register_pool_cleanup(pool)
280	            # Bootstrap the schema once per process per conn_str. The advisory lock and the DDL
281	            # must run on the SAME connection (pg_advisory_lock is session-scoped); a pool-backed
282	            # setup() would lock one connection and DDL on another, leaving the lock ineffective.
283	            with pool.connection() as conn:
284	                _acquire_migration_lock(conn)  # bounded wait so a stuck peer can't hang us (#129)
285	                try:
286	                    # row_factory=dict_row makes this connection dict-rowed at runtime
287	                    # (what PostgresSaver requires); the cast asserts that runtime truth
288	                    # the type system can't infer from the pool kwargs.
289	                    PostgresSaver(cast(Any, conn)).setup()  # DDL on the locked connection
290	                finally:
291	                    # Best-effort unlock; the lock auto-releases when the connection
292	                    # returns to the pool, and raising here would mask a setup() error.
293	                    try:
294	                        conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_unlock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
295	                    except Exception:
296	                        pass
297	            with _init_lock:
298	                _POOLS_BY_CONN[conn_str] = pool  # cache for reuse on later calls (#144)
299	            return PostgresSaver(cast(Any, pool))  # dict-rowed pool (row_factory=dict_row)
300	        except Exception as exc:
301	            # Build the redacted message here, but raise it OUTSIDE the handler (below) so the
302	            # original — credential-bearing — exception is retained as neither __cause__ NOR
303	            # __context__ (a logger walking __context__ would otherwise re-expose the password).
304	            failure = (
305	                f"Postgres checkpointer connection failed (is Postgres running on "
306	                f"{_sanitize_conn_str(conn_str)}?): {redact_creds(str(exc), conn_str)}. "
307	                "For tests without Postgres, use get_checkpointer(use_memory=True)."
308	            )
309	            # The pool may have opened before bootstrap failed — close it and drop it from the
310	            # cleanup registry so a failed init does not leak the connection/worker thread (#105).
311	            if pool is not None:
312	                _safe_close(pool)
313	                with _init_lock:
314	                    if pool in _OPEN_POOLS:
315	                        _OPEN_POOLS.remove(pool)
316	        raise RuntimeError(failure)
317	
318	
319	def configured_checkpointer(use_memory: bool | None = None) -> Any:
320	    """Return the checkpointer the environment selects.
321	
322	    ``use_memory`` (or ``ASSAY_CHECKPOINT_BACKEND=memory``) chooses the in-memory saver;
323	    otherwise Postgres.
324	    """
325	    if use_memory is None:
326	        use_memory = os.environ.get("ASSAY_CHECKPOINT_BACKEND", "postgres").lower() == "memory"
327	    return get_checkpointer(use_memory=use_memory)
328

1	"""Tiered reasoning seam — the single abstraction over LLM execution by stakes tier.
2	
3	No engine component talks to an LLM directly; everything routes through :class:`ReasoningSeam`.
4	Ported and generalized from the prior platform's hardened ``reasoning_client`` (ARCHITECTURE.md
5	§1, ADR-0003). Two execution tiers:
6	
7	- ``StakesTier.BULK`` — a local model runtime (loopback-only, e.g. Ollama) for high-volume,
8	  low-stakes work.
9	- ``StakesTier.HIGH_STAKES`` — a frontier model via a **fixed-cost subscription** path
10	  (the ``claude`` CLI / Agent SDK using an OAuth token). No metered API key is ever used:
11	  every metered Anthropic credential is scrubbed from the subprocess environment first.
12	
13	Hardened invariants preserved from the prior platform:
14	
15	- forward-proof credential scrubbing (any ``ANTHROPIC_*KEY``/``*TOKEN`` stripped; the
16	  subscription ``CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`` preserved);
17	- hard per-call timeouts via a bounded worker pool with a saturation guard;
18	- independent retry budgets — exponential backoff for transient errors, long backpressure
19	  for rate limits — and permanent errors never retried;
20	- robust JSON extraction via a balanced-brace walker (not a greedy regex);
21	- loopback enforcement on the local backend (ADR-0003).
22	
23	Heavy backends (``langchain_ollama``, ``claude_agent_sdk``) and tracing (``opentelemetry``)
24	are imported lazily, so this module imports — and is unit-testable by mocking ``_attempt`` —
25	with none of them installed. Install the optional ``reasoning`` extra to run real calls.
26	"""
27	
28	from __future__ import annotations
29	
30	import atexit
31	import json
32	import os
33	import threading
34	import time
35	from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor
36	from concurrent.futures import TimeoutError as FuturesTimeout
37	from contextlib import contextmanager
38	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
39	from enum import Enum
40	from pathlib import Path
41	from typing import Any, Callable, Iterator, Mapping, Protocol, cast
42	
43	from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping
44	from assay_engine._local import require_loopback_url
45	
46	
47	class StakesTier(Enum):
48	    BULK = "bulk"  # local model — high volume, low stakes
49	    HIGH_STAKES = "high"  # frontier model via fixed-cost subscription — gated, traced
50	
51	
52	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
53	class ReasoningRequest:
54	    prompt: str
55	    tier: StakesTier
56	    purpose: str
57	    params: Mapping[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
58	
59	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
60	        object.__setattr__(self, "params", freeze_mapping(self.params))
61	
62	
63	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
64	# Errors
65	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
66	class ReasoningError(RuntimeError):
67	    """Transient reasoning failure (eligible for retry)."""
68	
69	
70	class PermanentReasoningError(ReasoningError):
71	    """Non-transient failure — never retried (bad model, auth, saturation)."""
72	
73	
74	class RateLimitError(ReasoningError):
75	    """Rate-limit / capacity signal — retried with long backpressure, not fast backoff."""
76	
77	
78	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
79	# Config (dataset-agnostic env contract, ASSAY_* prefix)
80	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
81	def _env(name: str, default: str) -> str:
82	    val = os.environ.get(name)
83	    return val if val is not None and val != "" else default
84	
85	
86	def _truthy(name: str) -> bool:
87	    return os.environ.get(name, "").strip().lower() in {"1", "true", "yes", "on"}
88	
89	
90	BULK_MODEL = _env("ASSAY_BULK_MODEL", "llama3.1:8b")
91	BULK_BASE_URL = _env("ASSAY_BULK_BASE_URL", "http://localhost:11434")

117	# Credential scrubbing (ADR-0003 / no metered API) — pure, forward-proof

119	def is_metered_anthropic_credential(key: str) -> bool:
120	    """True for any env var the Anthropic SDK would read as a METERED credential.
121	
122	    Matches the *shape* — any ``ANTHROPIC_*`` var naming a ``KEY`` or ``TOKEN`` — rather than
123	    a static list, so a newly-added metered var cannot silently slip through. The subscription
124	    ``CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`` does not start with ``ANTHROPIC_`` and is deliberately kept.
125	
126	    The match is case-insensitive (pass 3, #F-003): a metered key set under a non-uppercase name
127	    (``anthropic_api_key``) must scrub too — the AWS_/GOOGLE_ checks already uppercase, so this
128	    closes the one credential path that did not.
129	    """
130	    ku = key.upper()
131	    return ku.startswith("ANTHROPIC_") and ("KEY" in ku or "TOKEN" in ku)

134	# Env vars that — beyond metered credentials — would redirect the subprocess OFF-BOX or onto a
135	# metered provider, defeating ADR-0003 / "no metered API". The claude CLI honours all of these.
136	_UNSAFE_SUBPROCESS_VARS = frozenset(
137	    {
138	        "ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL",
139	        "ANTHROPIC_BEDROCK_BASE_URL",
140	        "ANTHROPIC_VERTEX_BASE_URL",
141	        "CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK",
142	        "CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX",
143	        "CLAUDE_CODE_API_KEY_HELPER",
144	        "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS",
145	    }
146	)

149	def is_unsafe_subprocess_var(key: str) -> bool:
150	    """True for any env var that must not reach the high-stakes subprocess (#101).
151	
152	    Covers metered credentials, off-box/metered-provider redirects, proxies (which redirect
153	    egress), and cloud-provider credentials (AWS_*/GOOGLE_*). The subscription OAuth token is
154	    deliberately NOT matched.
155	    """
156	    ku = key.upper()
157	    return (
158	        is_metered_anthropic_credential(key)
159	        or ku in _UNSAFE_SUBPROCESS_VARS
160	        or ku.endswith("_PROXY")  # HTTP_PROXY/HTTPS_PROXY/ALL_PROXY/NO_PROXY (any case)
161	        or ku.startswith("AWS_")
162	        or ku.startswith("GOOGLE_")
163	    )

166	def scrubbed_env() -> dict[str, str]:
167	    """The process env hardened for the high-stakes subprocess (ADR-0003 / no metered API).
168	
169	    Every unsafe var (:func:`is_unsafe_subprocess_var`) is **overwritten to empty**, not merely
170	    omitted. The claude-agent-sdk builds the child env as ``{**os.environ, **options.env}`` — it
171	    *merges* over the inherited environment — so a dropped key would silently fall through to its
172	    inherited value (the metered key / off-box redirect would survive). Setting it to ``""`` in
173	    the dict we hand over guarantees the merge overwrites the inherited value (#101). The
174	    subscription OAuth token is preserved so the fixed-cost path still authenticates.
175	    """
176	    env = dict(os.environ)
177	    for k in list(env):
178	        if is_unsafe_subprocess_var(k):
179	            env[k] = ""
180	    return env

183	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
184	# Bounded timeout pool with saturation guard
185	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
186	_POOL_WORKERS = 8
187	_pool = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=_POOL_WORKERS, thread_name_prefix="reasoning-timeout")
188	# Shut the pool down at interpreter exit (#F-043). wait=False avoids blocking exit on in-flight
189	# backend calls (already bounded by _with_timeout); without this, module globals can be torn down
190	# while a worker is mid-call, leaving behavior undefined. Mirrors checkpoint.py's atexit cleanup.
191	atexit.register(_pool.shutdown, wait=False)
192	_inflight = 0
193	_inflight_lock = threading.Lock()
194	
195	
196	def _release(future: Any) -> None:
197	    # Idempotent per future (#F-032 follow-up): decrement _inflight AT MOST ONCE for a given
198	    # future. The done-callback path and the interrupt-window fallback in _submit_bounded can BOTH
199	    # reach _release for the same future when add_done_callback appended the callback and then was
200	    # interrupted before returning — without this guard that double-decrements and over-releases a
201	    # slot (the inverse of the leak being fixed). A per-future flag set under the lock makes the
202	    # "_release runs at most once" claim true rather than aspirational.
203	    global _inflight
204	    with _inflight_lock:
205	        if getattr(future, "_assay_released", False):
206	            return
207	        try:
208	            future._assay_released = True
209	        except (AttributeError, TypeError):  # pragma: no cover - Future allows attrs; defensive
210	            pass
211	        _inflight -= 1
212	
213	
214	def _submit_bounded(fn: Callable[[], Any]) -> Any:
215	    """Submit to the shared pool, refusing if every worker slot is already in flight
216	    (hung backend calls would otherwise leak all slots)."""
217	    global _inflight
218	    with _inflight_lock:
219	        if _inflight >= _POOL_WORKERS:
220	            # Saturation is a TRANSIENT condition — surface a retryable backpressure signal so
221	            # _run_with_retries waits and re-tries rather than failing the caller permanently
222	            # (#104). Concurrent callers thus queue briefly instead of erroring out.
223	            raise RateLimitError(
224	                f"reasoning timeout pool saturated ({_inflight}/{_POOL_WORKERS} in-flight) — "
225	                "backpressure; retry shortly"
226	            )
227	        _inflight += 1
228	    try:
229	        future = _pool.submit(fn)
230	    except RuntimeError as exc:  # pool shut down at interpreter exit — normalize, don't leak (#116)
231	        with _inflight_lock:
232	            _inflight -= 1
233	        raise PermanentReasoningError(f"reasoning pool is shut down: {exc}") from exc
234	    except BaseException:
235	        with _inflight_lock:
236	            _inflight -= 1
237	        raise
238	    # Register the slot-release callback under its own guard (#F-032): if a BaseException
239	    # (KeyboardInterrupt/SystemExit) fires while attaching the callback, release the slot
240	    # explicitly so a permanent leak can't accumulate and brick the pool with spurious
241	    # RateLimitErrors. If the interrupt landed AFTER add_done_callback already appended the
242	    # callback (so it will still fire on completion), the explicit _release here and the later
243	    # callback both target this future — _release is per-future idempotent, so the slot is
244	    # released exactly once either way.
245	    try:
246	        future.add_done_callback(_release)
247	    except BaseException:
248	        _release(future)
249	        raise
250	    return future
251	
252	
253	def _with_timeout(fn: Callable[[], Any], timeout: float, what: str) -> Any:
254	    """Run a sync callable with a hard wall-clock timeout via the bounded pool."""
255	    future = _submit_bounded(fn)
256	    try:
257	        return future.result(timeout=timeout)
258	    except FuturesTimeout as exc:
259	        # No future.cancel(): a ThreadPoolExecutor cannot interrupt a thread already blocked
260	        # in a backend call. The worker keeps its slot until the call returns; the bounded
261	        # pool's saturation guard is the real protection against leaked/hung slots.
262	        raise ReasoningError(f"{what} timed out after {timeout:.0f}s") from exc

265	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
266	# JSON extraction — balanced-brace walker (robust to fences / trailing prose)
267	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
268	def _first_balanced_json(text: str) -> str | None:
269	    """Return the first balanced ``{...}`` or ``[...]`` span, respecting string literals.
270	
271	    A greedy regex grabs from the first brace to the LAST brace, mangling replies with
272	    multiple JSON values or braces in trailing prose; this walks the structure honestly.
273	    """
274	    start = None
275	    opener = closer = ""
276	    depth = 0
277	    in_string = False
278	    escape = False
279	    for i, ch in enumerate(text):
280	        if start is None:
281	            if ch in "{[":
282	                start, opener = i, ch
283	                closer = "}" if ch == "{" else "]"
284	                depth = 1
285	            continue
286	        if in_string:
287	            if escape:
288	                escape = False
289	            elif ch == "\\":
290	                escape = True
291	            elif ch == '"':
292	                in_string = False
293	            continue
294	        if ch == '"':
295	            in_string = True
296	        elif ch == opener:
297	            depth += 1
298	        elif ch == closer:
299	            depth -= 1
300	            if depth == 0:
301	                return text[start : i + 1]
302	    return None
303	
304	
305	def extract_json(text: str) -> Any:
306	    """Parse JSON from a model reply (handles code fences and surrounding prose)."""
307	    span = _first_balanced_json(text)
308	    if span is None:
309	        raise ReasoningError("no JSON object/array found in reply")
310	    try:
311	        return json.loads(span)
312	    except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
313	        raise ReasoningError(f"reply contained malformed JSON: {exc}") from exc

316	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
317	# Tier backends (lazy imports — module loads without them)
318	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
319	def _bulk_complete(
320	    prompt: str,
321	    system: str | None,
322	    temperature: float,
323	    model: str,
324	    json_mode: bool = False,
325	    *,
326	    seed: int | None = None,
327	    json_schema: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
328	) -> str:
329	    """Tier: BULK — local model runtime (loopback-enforced).
330	
331	    ``num_predict`` is always set (bounded generation). For JSON, a caller-supplied
332	    ``json_schema`` uses Ollama's schema-constrained decoding (the reliable path); otherwise
333	    ``json_mode`` falls back to loose ``format="json"``. ``seed`` lets a retry force a different
334	    deterministic decode without sharpening temperature.
335	    """
336	    require_loopback_url(BULK_BASE_URL, what="bulk-tier model base URL")
337	    try:
338	        from langchain_ollama import ChatOllama
339	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
340	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
341	            "bulk tier requires the 'reasoning' extra (langchain-ollama) — not installed"
342	        ) from exc
343	
344	    kwargs: dict[str, Any] = dict(
345	        model=model,
346	        base_url=BULK_BASE_URL,
347	        temperature=temperature,
348	        num_predict=BULK_NUM_PREDICT,  # bound generation length (H1)
349	        client_kwargs={"timeout": BULK_TIMEOUT},
350	    )
351	    if seed is not None:
352	        kwargs["seed"] = seed
353	    if json_schema is not None:
354	        kwargs["format"] = json_schema  # Ollama schema-constrained decoding (reliable JSON)
355	    elif json_mode:
356	        kwargs["format"] = "json"  # loose native JSON decoding (syntactic only)
357	    client = ChatOllama(**kwargs)
358	    messages: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
359	    if system:
360	        messages.append(("system", system))
361	    messages.append(("human", prompt))
362	    try:
363	        reply = client.invoke(messages)
364	    except Exception as exc:
365	        # Prefer the typed HTTP status (ollama.ResponseError / httpx carry one): 404 = the model
366	        # isn't pulled → permanent. Fall back to substring matching only if no status is exposed
367	        # (robust to upstream message-wording changes — audit M4).
368	        status = getattr(exc, "status_code", None) or getattr(
369	            getattr(exc, "response", None), "status_code", None
370	        )
371	        msg = str(exc).lower()
372	        permanent = status == 404 or any(
373	            s in msg for s in ("not found", "no such model", "try pulling")
374	        )
375	        if permanent:
376	            raise PermanentReasoningError(f"bulk model {model!r} not available: {exc}") from exc
377	        raise ReasoningError(f"bulk tier call failed: {exc}") from exc
378	    content = getattr(reply, "content", reply)
379	    text = content if isinstance(content, str) else str(content)
380	    text = text.strip()
381	    if not text:  # an empty reply is not a valid result — retry, don't return "" (issue #5/R5)
382	        raise ReasoningError("bulk tier returned an empty reply")
383	    return text
384

387	    """Subscription auth available: an exported OAuth token OR a stored CLI credentials file
388	    (the normal state after ``claude login`` without exporting a token)."""
389	    if os.environ.get(OAUTH_TOKEN_ENV):
390	        return True
391	    return (Path.home() / ".claude" / ".credentials.json").is_file()

394	def _high_stakes_complete(prompt: str, system: str | None, model: str | None) -> str:
395	    """Tier: HIGH_STAKES — frontier model via subscription CLI/Agent SDK (no metered key)."""
396	    try:
397	        import anyio
398	        from claude_agent_sdk import (
399	            AssistantMessage,
400	            ClaudeAgentOptions,
401	            ResultMessage,
402	            TextBlock,
403	            query,
404	        )
405	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
406	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
407	            "high-stakes tier requires the 'reasoning' extra (claude-agent-sdk) — not installed"
408	        ) from exc
409	    if not _high_stakes_auth_present():
410	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
411	            f"high-stakes tier requires subscription auth ({OAUTH_TOKEN_ENV} or a stored "
412	            "~/.claude/.credentials.json); none found"
413	        )
414	
415	    options = ClaudeAgentOptions(
416	        system_prompt=system,
417	        model=model,
418	        permission_mode="dontAsk",
419	        setting_sources=[],
420	        allowed_tools=[],
421	        env=scrubbed_env(),  # ADR-0003: never pass a metered Anthropic credential
422	    )
423	
424	    parts: list[str] = []
425	    result_error: dict[str, Any] | None = None
426	
427	    async def _drain() -> None:
428	        # The CLI/SDK signals rate windows and API failures by COMPLETING the stream
429	        # (a RateLimitEvent, or a terminal ResultMessage with is_error) — NOT by raising.
430	        # We must inspect the stream, not just catch exceptions (issue #R4).
431	        #
432	        # Inner cancellation (#127): wrap the stream in anyio.fail_after so a hung subprocess is
433	        # actually CANCELLED (the cancellation propagates into query() and tears down the child),
434	        # freeing the bounded-pool worker. The outer _with_timeout only raises in the caller; it
435	        # cannot cancel this thread, so without this a hung HIGH_STAKES call would leak its slot
436	        # permanently. Fires before the outer wall-clock (HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT + 30).
437	        nonlocal result_error
438	        with anyio.fail_after(HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT):
439	            await _drain_stream()
440	
441	    async def _drain_stream() -> None:
442	        nonlocal result_error
443	        async for msg in query(prompt=prompt, options=options):
444	            if type(msg).__name__ == "RateLimitEvent":
445	                rl = getattr(getattr(msg, "rate_limit_info", None), "status", None)
446	                if rl == "rejected":
447	                    result_error = {"status": 429, "detail": "RateLimitEvent rejected"}
448	                continue
449	            if getattr(msg, "status", None) == "rejected":
450	                result_error = {"status": 429, "detail": "rejected"}
451	                continue
452	            if isinstance(msg, AssistantMessage):
453	                for block in getattr(msg, "content", []) or []:
454	                    if isinstance(block, TextBlock):
455	                        parts.append(block.text)
456	            elif isinstance(msg, ResultMessage):
457	                # Do not let a trailing ResultMessage clobber an already-captured 429.
458	                if getattr(msg, "is_error", False) and not (
459	                    result_error and result_error.get("status") == 429
460	                ):
461	                    result_error = {
462	                        "status": getattr(msg, "api_error_status", None),
463	                        "detail": getattr(msg, "errors", None) or getattr(msg, "stop_reason", None),
464	                    }
465	
466	    try:
467	        anyio.run(_drain)
468	    except Exception as exc:
469	        raise ReasoningError(f"high-stakes tier call failed: {exc}") from exc
470	
471	    if result_error is not None:
472	        status = result_error.get("status")
473	        detail = result_error.get("detail") or status
474	        if status in (429, 529):
475	            raise RateLimitError(f"high-stakes tier rate window ({status}): {detail}")
476	        if status in (401, 403):
477	            raise PermanentReasoningError(f"high-stakes tier auth failed ({status}): {detail}")
478	        raise ReasoningError(f"high-stakes tier reported an error (status={status}): {detail}")
479	
480	    text = "".join(parts).strip()
481	    if not text:  # empty frontier reply is not a valid result — retry (issue #R5)
482	        raise ReasoningError("high-stakes tier returned an empty reply")
483	    return text

486	def _attempt(request: ReasoningRequest) -> str:
487	    """Route one request to its tier backend (no retry here)."""
488	    if _truthy("ASSAY_DISABLE_REASONING"):
489	        raise ReasoningError("reasoning is disabled (ASSAY_DISABLE_REASONING)")
490	    p = request.params
491	    system = p.get("system")
492	    temperature = float(p.get("temperature", 0.0))
493	    model = p.get("model")
494	    json_mode = bool(p.get("_json_mode", False))
495	    seed = p.get("_seed")
496	    json_schema = p.get("json_schema")  # optional caller schema → constrained JSON decoding
497	    if request.tier is StakesTier.BULK:
498	        return cast(
499	            str,
500	            _with_timeout(
501	                lambda: _bulk_complete(
502	                    request.prompt,
503	                    system,
504	                    temperature,
505	                    model or BULK_MODEL,
506	                    json_mode,
507	                    seed=int(seed) if seed is not None else None,
508	                    json_schema=json_schema,
509	                ),
510	                BULK_TIMEOUT + 10,
511	                "bulk tier",
512	            ),
513	        )
514	    if request.tier is StakesTier.HIGH_STAKES:
515	        # The high-stakes tier runs the Claude CLI subprocess (not the in-process Anthropic SDK,
516	        # so no auto-instrumentor covers it) — emit an explicit OpenInference LLM span so Phoenix
517	        # records the model/provider for this otherwise-untraced tier (#2).
518	        llm_attrs = {
519	            "llm.provider": "anthropic",
520	            "llm.system": "anthropic",
521	            "llm.model_name": str(model or HIGH_STAKES_MODEL or "subscription-default"),
522	        }
523	        with _span("reasoning.high_stakes", llm_attrs, kind="LLM"):
524	            return cast(
525	                str,
526	                _with_timeout(
527	                    lambda: _high_stakes_complete(
528	                        request.prompt, system, model or HIGH_STAKES_MODEL
529	                    ),
530	                    HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT + 30,
531	                    "high-stakes tier",
532	                ),
533	            )
534	    raise PermanentReasoningError(f"unknown tier: {request.tier!r}")

537	def _sleep_bounded(secs: float, deadline: float | None) -> None:
538	    """Sleep ``secs``, but never past ``deadline`` — so backoff can't blow an overall budget.
539	
540	    If the deadline has already passed, raise instead of sleeping the full interval, so a long
541	    rate-limit backoff cannot keep a caller blocked well beyond its deadline (#102).
542	    """
543	    if deadline is not None:
544	        remaining = deadline - time.monotonic()
545	        if remaining <= 0:
546	            raise ReasoningError("reasoning deadline exceeded during backoff")
547	        secs = min(secs, remaining)
548	    time.sleep(secs)
549	
550	
551	def _run_with_retries(request: ReasoningRequest, *, deadline: float | None = None) -> str:
552	    """Execute with independent transient and rate-limit retry budgets.
553	
554	    ``deadline`` (monotonic seconds) bounds both the cumulative backoff AND entry into each
555	    retry: once the deadline has passed, no further ``_attempt`` is started (#102, #F-022). It
556	    does not interrupt an attempt already in flight — that is bounded by the per-call timeout —
557	    so total wall-clock is at most ``deadline`` plus one in-flight attempt's timeout, not
558	    ``deadline`` plus the full retry budget of per-call timeouts.
559	    """
560	    transient = 0
561	    rate = 0
562	    attempted = False
563	    while True:
564	        # Gate retry ENTRY on the deadline (#F-022): the first attempt always runs, but a retry is
565	        # not started once the deadline is past — otherwise the per-attempt timeouts could stack
566	        # well beyond the overall budget even though the backoff sleeps were bounded.
567	        if attempted and deadline is not None and time.monotonic() >= deadline:
568	            raise ReasoningError("reasoning deadline exceeded before retry")
569	        attempted = True
570	        try:
571	            return _attempt(request)
572	        except PermanentReasoningError:
573	            raise
574	        except RateLimitError:
575	            if rate >= RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES:
576	                raise
577	            _sleep_bounded(RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF * (rate + 1), deadline)
578	            rate += 1
579	        except ReasoningError:
580	            if transient >= MAX_RETRIES:
581	                raise
582	            _sleep_bounded(BACKOFF_BASE**transient, deadline)
583	            transient += 1
584

590	class ReasoningSeam(Protocol):
591	    """Route a reasoning request to the appropriate tier with timeouts, retries, tracing."""
592

595	
596	@contextmanager
597	def _span(name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any], *, kind: str = "AGENT") -> Iterator[None]:
598	    """Emit an OpenTelemetry span if a provider is registered; a cheap no-op otherwise.
599	
600	    Stamps the OpenInference ``openinference.span.kind`` (default ``AGENT`` — a reasoning call)
601	    so Phoenix classifies the span instead of rendering it UNKNOWN. OpenTelemetry is imported
602	    lazily, so the seam traces when the (self-hosted, on-box) observability stack is wired and is
603	    a silent no-op in tests / offline (ADR-0003).
604	    """
605	    try:
606	        from opentelemetry import trace
607	    except ImportError:
608	        yield
609	        return
610	    tracer = trace.get_tracer("assay_engine.reasoning")
611	    # start_as_current_span auto-records exceptions + sets ERROR status by default (#F-005 was a
612	    # false positive — see assay_engine.observability.tracing for the verification note).
613	    with tracer.start_as_current_span(name) as span:
614	        span.set_attribute("openinference.span.kind", kind)
615	        for k, v in attributes.items():
616	            span.set_attribute(k, v)

content.text:
Confirmed a real residual issue: `set_config('lock_timeout', %s, false)` with `is_local=false` sets the value at **session scope**, which **persists** on the connection after it returns to the pool. Since this is the bootstrap connection (drawn from the same pool that PostgresSaver will later reuse for actual checkpoint writes), every subsequent checkpoint operation on that connection inherits the `lock_timeout`. psycopg_pool does NOT reset session state by default unless a `reset` callback is configured. This means a `lock_timeout` leaks into normal checkpoint operations, potentially causing checkpoint writes to abort under lock contention. Should be `true` (transaction-local) — but it's autocommit mode, so transaction-local won't survive to the `pg_advisory_lock` call. The correct fix is to RESET it after acquiring, or use a dedicated connection not returned to the pool.

This is a genuine, subtle residual leak of session state. Let me finalize my findings.

findings.title: lock_timeout session-GUC leaks into pooled connection used for all later checkpoint writes
findings.description: During schema bootstrap _acquire_migration_lock runs `SELECT set_config('lock_timeout', %s, false)` with is_local=false, i.e. SESSION scope. The connection is drawn from `pool` via `with pool.connection() as conn:` and, on block exit, is RETURNED to that same pool. psycopg_pool does NOT reset session state on check-in unless a `reset=` callback is configured on ConnectionPool (none is). The very same pool is then handed to PostgresSaver(pool) and used for every subsequent checkpoint read/write. So the bootstrap-only lock_timeout (default 30s) silently persists as a session setting on a long-lived pooled connection and applies to ordinary checkpoint operations. Under lock contention a normal checkpoint write that would otherwise block briefly will instead abort with 'canceling statement due to lock_timeout', i.e. durability loss at exactly the interrupt/resume the checkpointer exists to guarantee. is_local=true was avoided because under autocommit a transaction-local SET would not survive to the separate pg_advisory_lock statement — so the fix is to explicitly RESET after acquiring, or run bootstrap on a connection that is closed (not returned to the pool).
findings.poc: 1) ASSAY_MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS=30000 (default). 2) get_checkpointer() bootstraps; the bootstrap connection has lock_timeout='30s' set at session scope and is returned to the pool. 3) Two runs resume concurrently and contend on the same checkpoint row; a checkpoint UPDATE waits on the row lock >30s and raises 'canceling statement due to lock_timeout' instead of completing — a spurious durability failure. Reproduce by asserting `conn.execute("SHOW lock_timeout").fetchone()` is not '0' on a connection re-checked-out from the pool after bootstrap.
findings.suggested_fix: After PostgresSaver(conn).setup() succeeds (and the advisory unlock), reset the GUC on the same connection before block exit: `conn.execute("SELECT set_config('lock_timeout', '0', false)")` (or use RESET lock_timeout) in the finally block alongside the unlock; or configure ConnectionPool(reset=lambda c: c.execute('RESET ALL')); or open a dedicated one-shot psycopg.connect() for bootstrap that is closed rather than returned to the pool, so no bootstrap session state ever reaches checkpoint operations.

findings.title: BULK tier has no inner cancellation (asymmetric with HIGH_STAKES #127) — a hung backend permanently leaks a pool slot and drives endless retryable backpressure
findings.description: HIGH_STAKES wraps its work in `anyio.fail_after(HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT)` (#127) so a hung subprocess is actually CANCELLED inside the worker, freeing the bounded-pool slot. BULK has no equivalent: it relies solely on `client_kwargs={'timeout': BULK_TIMEOUT}` reaching httpx through ChatOllama, and on the outer `_with_timeout(..., BULK_TIMEOUT+10)` which, as its own docstring states, CANNOT interrupt a thread blocked in a backend call. If the httpx timeout does not fire (connection wedged mid-stream, a proxy holding the socket, ChatOllama not propagating client_kwargs on the streaming path, or a C-level blocking read), the worker thread is stuck forever and its _inflight slot is never released. Worse, because _submit_bounded reports saturation as a RETRYABLE RateLimitError and _run_with_retries treats RateLimitError as transient backpressure, once enough BULK workers wedge (up to _POOL_WORKERS=8) every future call loops on saturation backpressure that can never clear — a livelock that bricks the seam with no terminal error. The 3-pass hardening fixed the HIGH_STAKES leak but left the BULK path with the same defect it set out to close.
findings.poc: Monkeypatch _bulk_complete to call a blocking no-timeout read (simulate ChatOllama not honoring client_kwargs timeout): `def hang(): import threading; threading.Event().wait()`. Submit _POOL_WORKERS BULK requests via _with_timeout; each returns FuturesTimeout to the caller but the worker stays alive holding its slot. After 8 such, every subsequent _submit_bounded raises RateLimitError('saturated') indefinitely and _run_with_retries loops on backpressure forever (until RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES then PermanentReasoningError-by-exhaustion — but slots never recover for the rest of process life).
findings.suggested_fix: Give BULK the same inner, in-worker hard bound as HIGH_STAKES: run the ChatOllama call under an interruptible mechanism the worker itself can abort (e.g. run the generation inside `anyio.run` with `anyio.fail_after(BULK_TIMEOUT)` wrapping an async ollama call, or use the ollama async client; alternatively spawn the backend call in a cancellable subprocess). At minimum, document and enforce a watchdog that, on repeated saturation that never clears, surfaces a PermanentReasoningError ('reasoning pool wedged') rather than infinite RateLimitError backpressure, and add an explicit assertion that ChatOllama honors client_kwargs timeout on the streaming path.

findings.title: _release sets the idempotency flag on the Future under the lock but the done-callback may run before the flag attribute is settable, double-decrementing on AttributeError swallow
findings.description: _release relies on setting `future._assay_released = True` to be idempotent. The set is wrapped in `except (AttributeError, TypeError): pass` 'defensive' for Futures that disallow attribute assignment. concurrent.futures.Future DOES allow arbitrary attributes, so in practice the guard is fine — but the defensive branch creates a real correctness hole: if attribute assignment ever fails (a subclassed/slotted Future, or a test double), the early-return guard `getattr(future,'_assay_released',False)` can NEVER become True, so both the done-callback path AND the _submit_bounded interrupt-fallback path will each pass the guard and execute `_inflight -= 1`, over-releasing the slot. Over-release lets _inflight drift negative, defeating the saturation guard (more than _POOL_WORKERS concurrent submits) — the inverse of the leak the guard protects against. The 'at most once' invariant the comment claims is only true when attribute assignment succeeds.
findings.poc: Pass a Future-like object with __slots__ (no _assay_released slot) to _submit_bounded such that add_done_callback fires _release once and the BaseException fallback fires _release again. Each call's `future._assay_released = True` raises AttributeError (swallowed), the guard never trips, _inflight is decremented twice for one reserved slot, leaving _inflight negative.
findings.suggested_fix: Do not depend on per-object attribute assignment for idempotency. Track released futures in a set keyed by id() under _inflight_lock, or (simpler) make the done-callback the SOLE releaser and drop the interrupt-window fallback by reserving the slot only after add_done_callback is attached (submit, attach callback, then increment — or use a try/finally that the callback owns exclusively). If keeping the flag approach, make assignment failure fatal (assert) rather than silently swallowed, so the idempotency invariant cannot be violated.

findings.title: _CONN_INIT_LOCKS grows unboundedly — never pruned, keyed by raw conn_str
findings.description: _CONN_INIT_LOCKS is a process-lifetime dict keyed by the resolved conn_str and entries are only ever added (under _init_lock in _conn_init_lock), never removed. _POOLS_BY_CONN has the same add-only lifecycle. For a fixed deployment with one DB this is harmless, but get_postgres_connection_string() resolves from the ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL env each call, and conn_str distinctness includes any variation in the URL string (host alias, optional params, credentials rotation that changes the userinfo, sslmode toggles). Any workload that legitimately targets many distinct databases/DSNs over a long-lived process (multi-tenant runner, test harness rotating DSNs, credential rotation) accumulates a Lock object (and, for _POOLS_BY_CONN, a live ConnectionPool + worker thread) per distinct string with no eviction — an unbounded cache and, for pools, an unbounded thread/connection leak. Note credential rotation makes the SAME logical DB appear as a NEW conn_str, so the old pool is orphaned in _OPEN_POOLS (still atexit-closed, but never reused and never closed until exit).
findings.poc: In a loop, set os.environ['ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL'] to N distinct valid loopback DSNs (e.g. differing in an appended '?application_name=run_i') and call get_checkpointer() each iteration; observe len(cp._CONN_INIT_LOCKS) and len(cp._POOLS_BY_CONN) grow to N and the process worker-thread count climb by one per distinct DSN, none reclaimed.
findings.suggested_fix: Bound and/or key-normalize the caches: (a) canonicalize conn_str (drop volatile query params / strip userinfo for the cache key while keeping it for connect) so credential rotation and cosmetic param changes map to one entry; (b) cap _POOLS_BY_CONN with an LRU that closes (pool.close()) and removes the evicted pool from _OPEN_POOLS before discarding it; (c) prune _CONN_INIT_LOCKS for keys no longer in _POOLS_BY_CONN. This caps threads/connections and meta-dict growth in long-lived multi-DSN processes.

findings.title: HIGH_STAKES inner fail_after races the outer _with_timeout, and on cancellation parts/result_error are read after a partially-cancelled drain
findings.description: _high_stakes_complete runs `anyio.run(_drain)` inside a pool worker; _drain wraps _drain_stream in `anyio.fail_after(HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT)` while the outer _with_timeout uses `HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT + 30`. The two timers are close enough that under load (GC pause, scheduler delay) the outer FuturesTimeout can fire first; when it does, _with_timeout returns to the caller but the worker keeps running anyio.run until fail_after eventually cancels — the slot is held for up to ~30s extra (acceptable) BUT the bigger issue is correctness of shared state: parts (list) and result_error (dict) are mutated by the async iterator and read on the SAME worker thread after anyio.run returns, which is fine for the success path; however `anyio.fail_after` raises TimeoutError which is caught by the broad `except Exception` and re-raised as ReasoningError, discarding any partial parts already appended — so a near-complete reply that trips the inner timeout by one chunk is treated as a hard transient failure rather than salvaging captured text. More subtly, if query()'s cancellation does not actually tear down the subprocess (SDK swallows the cancel), the worker remains blocked past fail_after with no further timer, re-introducing the slot leak #127 claimed to fix. The two-timer design leaves no margin assertion that inner<outer with enough headroom.
findings.poc: Set HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT small; mock query() to yield N-1 of N chunks then sleep just over the inner timeout. Observe: parts holds N-1 partial chunks, fail_after raises, caught by `except Exception`, re-raised as ReasoningError; the partial text is dropped and the attempt counts as a transient failure. Separately, mock query() to ignore cancellation (await a shielded sleep) and observe the worker outlives fail_after, holding its _inflight slot.
findings.suggested_fix: (a) Make the timer hierarchy explicit and safe: assert/derive the outer timeout as inner + fixed headroom and document the worker may outlive the caller by that headroom. (b) Catch TimeoutError from fail_after distinctly from generic Exception so a near-complete reply can either be salvaged (if parts is non-empty) or raised as a clearly-typed timeout. (c) Harden cancellation: run query() in a structure that GUARANTEES subprocess teardown on cancel (e.g. anyio cancel scope + explicit process kill / context-manager close of the query stream), and add a test that a cancellation-ignoring backend still releases the slot.

findings.title: Bootstrap connection context-manager may COMMIT/return a connection left in a broken state if advisory-unlock and DDL both fail, with the pool reusing it
findings.description: In get_checkpointer, `with pool.connection() as conn:` acquires the advisory lock, runs setup() DDL, then best-effort unlocks in finally (swallowing errors). If setup() raises, the outer `except Exception` closes and de-registers the whole pool (good). But the unlock is swallowed unconditionally, and on autocommit the connection is then returned to the pool by the context manager even though it may still hold the session advisory lock (if pg_advisory_unlock failed e.g. due to a transient error) or carry the leaked lock_timeout GUC (see C-1). A returned connection that still holds the migration advisory lock will, on its next checkout for a real checkpoint op, keep blocking any OTHER process trying to bootstrap the same schema — the very cross-process serialization the lock provides is undermined because the lock is held by a connection now doing unrelated work. The auto-release-on-connection-return comment assumes the connection is CLOSED on return, but a pooled connection is reset/reused, not closed, so the session (and its advisory lock) persists.
findings.poc: Force pg_advisory_unlock to raise (monkeypatch conn.execute to throw only on the unlock statement). setup() succeeds, pool is cached and returned. The bootstrap connection retains the session advisory lock and is checked back into the pool. A second process calling get_checkpointer() on the same DB blocks on pg_advisory_lock for the full lock_timeout because process 1's pooled (idle) connection still holds it.
findings.suggested_fix: Do not rely on connection-return to release the session advisory lock — pooled connections are reset, not closed. Either (a) run bootstrap on a dedicated psycopg.connect() that is explicitly closed (closing the session truly releases the lock and discards GUCs — also fixes C-1), or (b) on unlock failure, mark the connection broken (conn.close() / pool.putconn(close=True)) so it is not returned to the pool holding the lock, and verify the unlock actually ran (check pg_advisory_unlock's boolean result) before treating it as released.

content.text:
I completed the adversarial concurrency and resource-leak audit of the two target files in `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay`. After three prior hardening passes, I found 6 residual issues (2 high, 3 medium, 1 low). Key files:

- `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/persistence/checkpoint.py`
- `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py`

Highest-impact residuals:

**C-1 (high)** — `_acquire_migration_lock` sets `lock_timeout` at SESSION scope (`set_config(..., false)`) on a connection drawn from the shared pool. psycopg_pool has no `reset=` callback configured, so that bootstrap-only GUC persists on the pooled connection and applies to every later checkpoint write — turning brief lock contention into spurious `canceling statement due to lock_timeout` durability failures. (Line 212.)

**C-2 (high)** — The BULK tier never got the inner in-worker cancellation that HIGH_STAKES received in #127. It relies only on `client_kwargs timeout` reaching httpx plus the outer `_with_timeout` (which by its own docstring cannot interrupt a blocked worker). A wedged BULK call leaks its `_inflight` slot permanently; once enough leak, `_submit_bounded` returns retryable `RateLimitError` saturation forever — a livelock that bricks the seam with no terminal error. (Line 253.)

The medium/low findings cover the unbounded `_CONN_INIT_LOCKS`/`_POOLS_BY_CONN` growth under DSN/credential rotation (C-4), the `_release` idempotency flag's silent-swallow over-release hole on slotted Futures (C-3), the two-timer inner/outer race in HIGH_STAKES that drops near-complete partial replies and can still leak a slot if the SDK ignores cancellation (C-5), and the bootstrap connection being returned to the pool potentially still holding the session advisory lock when unlock fails, since pooled connections are reset not closed (C-6).

Full details, PoCs, and suggested fixes were returned via the StructuredOutput tool.
